perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Nevada, USA in force therein without regard to its conflict of law rules. All parties agree that by executing this Agreement they have attorned to the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court of the United States. The Supreme Court shall have exclusive jurisdiction over this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Nevada, USA in force therein without regard to its conflict of law rules. All parties agree that by executing this Agreement they have attorned to the jurisdiction of the Nevada Small Claims Court. The Small Claims Court shall have exclusive jurisdiction over this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The contract originally specifies that the Supreme Court of the United States has exclusive jurisdiction. This has been changed to the Nevada Small Claims Court. This is a contradiction as small claims courts have jurisdictional limits based on the amount in dispute, and the US Supreme Court has no jurisdiction over contract disputes unless a federal question is involved.
perturbation.law_explanation: NRS 4.370 defines the jurisdiction of Nevada Small Claims Courts, limiting it based on the amount in controversy. Giving exclusive jurisdiction to the Small Claims Court, regardless of the amount, contradicts this statute.

perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a legal contradiction. The original contract designates the Supreme Court of the United States as having exclusive jurisdiction, while the modified text gives this power to the Nevada Small Claims Court. The Law Explanation states that NRS 4.370 defines the jurisdiction of Nevada Small Claims Courts, limiting it based on the amount in controversy. The Perturbation Explanation notes that small claims courts have jurisdictional limits based on the amount in dispute. This contradicts the original text since the US Supreme Court has no jurisdiction over contract disputes unless a federal question is involved. Scraped Law Snippet 2, lists NRS 4.370, further validating that Nevada Small Claims Court jurisdiction is codified in Nevada law. By assigning exclusive jurisdiction to the Small Claims Court, the changed text contradicts the established limits on its jurisdiction, as supported by the Law Explanation. Therefore a legal contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Dolphin shall maintain at its principal place of business, or such other place as may be most convenient, separate accounts and records of all services provided to DGT, such accounts and records to be in sufficient detail to enable proper returns to be made under this Agreement. Dolphin shall deliver to DGT on the date 30 days after each and every year a detailed photo-editing report on all services provided on a unit basis (i.e. customer number, file number, cost). Page 2 of 5 The calculation shall be carried out in accordance with generally accepted U.S. accounting principles ("GAAP"), or the standards and principles adopted by the U.S. Financial Accounting Standards Board ("FASB") applied on a consistent basis.
perturbation.changed_text: Dolphin shall maintain at its principal place of business, or such other place as may be most convenient, separate accounts and records of all services provided to DGT, such accounts and records to be in sufficient detail to enable proper returns to be made under this Agreement. Dolphin shall deliver to DGT on the date 30 days after each and every year a detailed photo-editing report on all services provided on a unit basis (i.e. customer number, file number, cost). Page 2 of 5 The calculation shall be carried out based on principles at the discretion of Dolphin.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract specifies that accounting must be carried out in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) or standards adopted by the FASB. This has been changed to allow Dolphin to use principles at their discretion. This contradicts the established accounting standards and would make financial reporting unreliable.
perturbation.law_explanation: 17 CFR § 210.4-01 requires financial statements filed with the SEC to be prepared in accordance with GAAP. While this contract is not necessarily related to SEC filings, the explicit deviation from GAAP creates uncertainty and potential conflict with established accounting regulations, if the information ends up being used in a financial statement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 210.4-01 - Form, order, and terminology. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 210.4-01 - Form, order, and terminology. CFR prev | next § 210.4-01 Form, order, and terminology. (a) Financial statements should be filed in such form and order, and should use such generally accepted terminology, as will best indicate their significance and character in the light of the provisions applicable thereto. The information required with respect to any statement shall be furnished as a minimum requirement to which shall be added such further material information as is necessary to make the required statements, in the light of the circumstances under which they are made, not misleading. (1) Financial statements filed with the Commission which are not prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles will be presumed to be misleading or inaccurate, despite footnote or other disclosures, unless the Commission has otherwise provided. This article and other articles of Regulation S-X provide clarification of certain disclosures which must be included in any event, in financial statements filed with the Commission. (2) In all filings of foreign private issuers (see § 230.405 of this chapter), except as stated otherwise in the applicable form, the financial statements may be prepared according to a comprehensive set of accounting principles, other than those generally accepted in the United States or International Financial Reporting Standards as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board, if a reconciliation to U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and the provisions of Regulation S-X of the type specified in Item 18 of Form 20-F (§ 249.220f of this chapter) is also filed as part of the financial statements . Alternatively, the financial statements may be prepared according to U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles or International

perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the requirement to use GAAP or FASB standards with a discretionary approach, stating that calculations will be based on principles at the discretion of Dolphin. This directly contradicts the principle in 17 CFR § 210.4-01(a)(1) which states that financial statements filed with the Commission not prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles will be presumed to be misleading or inaccurate. The original contract mandated GAAP or FASB; the changed contract allows Dolphin to use any principles it chooses, creating a high likelihood of non-compliance if these statements are filed with the SEC.

perturbation.original_text: DGT shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement if Dolphin fails to meet its requirements and material obligation hereunder and only after DGT have given Dolphin written notice of such failure and only after Dolphin has not rectified such failure within 10 days of the notice. Dolphin shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement only after DGT fails to meet a material obligation hereunder and only after Dolphin has given DGT written notice of such failure and only after the DGT has not rectified such failure within 30 days of the notice.
perturbation.changed_text: DGT shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement if Dolphin, in DGT's sole opinion, fails to meet its requirements and obligation hereunder, with no prior notice required. Dolphin shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement only after DGT fails to meet a material obligation hereunder and only after Dolphin has given DGT written notice of such failure and only after the DGT has not rectified such failure within 30 days of the notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract requires written notice and a 10-day period for Dolphin to rectify a failure before DGT can terminate. This has been changed to allow DGT to terminate the agreement immediately and based on its sole opinion without prior notice. This change creates a legal contradiction by making the termination clause unreasonably favorable to one party.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-309(3) states that termination of a contract by one party requires reasonable notification. This change allows DGT to terminate without any notice, potentially violating this requirement, as photo editing can be considered as 'goods' under the UCC because the ultimate purpose is the sale of digital images. Even if not 'goods' directly, principles of good faith and fair dealing, which underpin commercial law, suggest such a clause is potentially problematic.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-309. Absence of Specific Time Provisions; Notice of Termination. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-309. Absence of Specific Time Provisions; Notice of Termination. (1) The time for shipment or delivery or any other action under a contract if not provided in this Article or agreed upon shall be a reasonable time. (2) Where the contract provides for successive performances but is indefinite in duration it is valid for a reasonable time but unless otherwise agreed may be terminated at any time by either party. (3) Termination of a contract by one party except on the happening of an agreed event requires that reasonable notification be received by the other party and an agreement dispensing with notification is invalid if its operation would be unconscionable. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows DGT to terminate the agreement without prior notice, which contradicts UCC § 2-309(3), as highlighted in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states, 'Termination of a contract by one party except on the happening of an agreed event requires that reasonable notification be received by the other party and an agreement dispensing with notification is invalid if its operation would be unconscionable.' The modified contract removes the requirement for 'reasonable notification,' making it likely unconscionable as it allows termination at DGT's 'sole opinion' without any prior warning. The original text ensured a notice period, while the changed text eliminates this protection, creating a contradiction with the legal requirement for reasonable notification.
